revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:弘前ねぷた]][[ko:히로사키 네부타]]



{{Infobox_holiday
| Event Name = Hirosaki Neputa Festival
| English Title = 
| image = File:Ōgi Neputa 2.jpg
| image Size = 300px
| image Caption = Ōgi Neputa (Fan Neputa) at the Hirosaki Neputa Festival (Front Painting)
| Type = {{ill|Festival|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132241}}
| Nickname = 
| Official Name = 
| Former Name = 
| Schedule = {{ill|August 1|en|August 1|qid=Q2788|lt=August 1|1=August 1}} - {{ill|August 7|en|August 7|qid=Q2781|lt=August 7|1=August 7}}
| First Held = 
| Last Held = 
| Time = 
| Venue = {{ill|Hirosaki|en|Hirosaki|qid=Q194087|lt=Hirosaki City|1=Hirosaki}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|qid=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}
| Organizer = 
| Co-Sponsor = 
| Support =

| Parking = 
| URL = 
| Notes = 
}}

[[File:Ōgi Neputa 3.jpg|thumb|Ōgi Neputa (Rear Painting) at the Hirosaki Neputa Festival]]
The '''Hirosaki Neputa Festival''' (弘前ねぷたまつり) is a {{ill|Neputa|ja|ねぶた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neputa|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q861941}} held annually in {{ill|Hirosaki|en|Hirosaki|qid=Q194087|lt=Hirosaki City|1=Hirosaki}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|qid=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}.

It is one of the "Four Great Festivals of Hirosaki," along with the {{ill|Hirosaki Cherry Blossom Festival|ja|弘前さくらまつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirosaki Cherry Blossom Festival|1=Hirosaki Cherry Blossom Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487199}}, {{ill|Hirosaki Castle Chrysanthemum and Autumn Leaves Festival|ja|弘前城菊と紅葉まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirosaki Castle Chrysanthemum and Autumn Leaves Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hirosaki Castle Snow Lantern Festival|ja|弘前城雪燈籠まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirosaki Castle Snow Lantern Festival|1=Hirosaki Castle Snow Lantern Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487218}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals|url=https://www.hirosaki-kanko.or.jp/cat07.html|website=Hirosaki Tourism and Convention Bureau|accessdate=October 10, 2022|language=ja}}</ref> During the festival, crowds of citizens parade through the streets chanting "Yah Yado~" (ヤーヤドー) while pulling floats decorated with warrior paintings. Hirosaki Neputa features '''Ōgi Neputa''' (扇ねぷた, fan-shaped floats) and '''Kumi Neputa''' (組ねぷた, doll-shaped floats), with approximately 80 floats in total—the highest number in Aomori Prefecture.<ref name=":10" />

== Origins ==

There are several theories about the origins of the Hirosaki Neputa Festival:<ref name=":1">{{Cite news|title=Hirosaki Neputa Restarts Unified Processions After 3 Years Amid Its 300th Anniversary|url=https://kahoku.news/articles/20220722khn000025.html|newspaper=Kahoku Shinpo|date=July 25, 2022|accessdate=October 9, 2022}}</ref>
* '''Legend''': In the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|qid=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}}, a {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|qid=Q131767|lt=Sei-i Taishōgun|1=Shogun}}, created large dolls to lure out the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|qid=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}.
* '''Tradition''': In {{ill|1593|en|1593|qid=Q6772|lt=1593|1=1593}} (Bunroku 2), {{ill|Tsugaru Tamenobu|en|Tsugaru Tamenobu|qid=Q909029|lt=Tsugaru Tamenobu|1=Tsugaru Tamenobu}}, the founder of the domain, constructed a large lantern during his stay in Kyoto for the {{ill|Urabon-e|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabon-e|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q15898039}}.
* '''Accepted Theory''': The current form of Neputa evolved from the fusion of Edo-period customs such as {{ill|Qixi Festival|en|Qixi Festival|qid=Q13423122|lt=Tanabata|1=Qixi Festival}} fire rituals, spirit-sending ({{ill|Spirit_Boat_Procession|en|Spirit_Boat_Procession|qid=Q7577865|lt=Spirit Boat Procession|1=Spirit Boat Procession}}), and "Nemuri Nagashi" (眠り流し), as well as {{ill|Bon lanterns|ja|盆燈籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bon lanterns|1=Bon-dōrō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581147}}.

The term "Neputa" is thought to derive from "Nemuri Nagashi" (眠り流し → ねむた流し → ねむた → ねぷた). These customs, including the "Nemuri Nagashi," were widely practiced across Japan.<ref name=":1" />

It was originally a ritual to drive away drowsiness and misfortune during the peak agricultural season and send them out of the village with water.<ref name=":1" />

== History ==

=== 1593 (Bunroku 2) ===
The founder of the Tsugaru clan, Tsugaru Tamenobu, organized the procession of a large lantern measuring two ken (approximately 3.6 meters) on each side at the Tsugaru residence in Kyoto as part of the Obon festival.<ref>"Tsugaru Henran Nikki" (1792, written by Kidadi Yōzaemon)</ref>

=== 1682 (Tenpō 2) ===
During the reign of the 4th domain lord, Tsugaru Nobumasa, the first festival float (dashi) was paraded as a forerunner to the portable shrine (mikoshi) during the Hirosaki Hachiman Shrine festival.<ref name=":2" /> <ref group="Note">This influenced the later formation of the Hirosaki-style "Kumi Neputa" and the parade order of various neighborhoods.</ref>

=== 1720 (Kyōhō 5), July 6 (Lunisolar: August 9) ===
The 5th domain lord, Tsugaru Nobuhisa, viewed the "Nemuri Nagashi" procession at Hōon-ji Temple in Shintera Town.<ref name=":2">"Hirosaki Clan Administrative Diary" (Onkuni Nikki)</ref>

=== 1722 (Kyōhō 7), July 6 (Lunisolar: August 17) ===
Tsugaru Nobuhisa viewed the "Nebuta Nagashi" parade in the weaving district of the castle town. The parade order was: 1) Honmachi, Oyakata-machi, Kaji-machi; 2) Momori-machi; 3) Dote-machi; 4) Higashinaga-machi, Hontera-machi; 5) Watoku-machi; 6) Konya-machi; 7) Kamekō-machi, Tamogi-machi; 8) Ara-machi. The parade moved from Konya-machi to Kasuga-machi, and Nobuhisa returned around 8 PM.<ref name=":2" />{{Refnest|group="Note"|This is the first recorded mention of "Neputa" in surviving historical documents.<ref name=":1" />}}

=== 1727 (Kyōhō 12) ===
A memorandum stated that due to financial difficulties, the viewing of "Nemuta" dance performances was canceled. Additionally, it was instructed that the festivals in Aomori and Ajigasawa be limited to only mikoshi processions. The magistrates of Hirosaki, Aomori, and Ajigasawa were notified.<ref name=":2" />

=== 1728 (Kyōhō 13), July 6 (Lunisolar: August 11) ===
A memorandum warned against pRanks during Obon celebrations, such as cutting down children's lanterns. That evening was the scheduled night for "Nemuta Nagashi" by children throughout Hirosaki, and officials were instructed to maintain order.<ref name=":2" />

=== 1738 (Genbun 1) ===
The 6th domain lord, Tsugaru Nobutomo, viewed festival floats from the Tatsumi Yagura (current-day Tenshu) at the Hirosaki Hachiman Shrine festival.<ref name=":2" />

=== 1739 (Genbun 4), July 6 (Lunisolar: August 10) ===
A directive was issued to prevent children from throwing stones, brandishing wooden swords, or engaging in quarrels during the "Nebuta Nagashi" procession.<ref name=":2" />

=== 1748 (Enkyō 5), July 4 (Lunisolar: July 28) ===
A decree was issued banning "Nebuta Nagashi" except on July 6 (Lunisolar). Additionally, residents were warned against disorderly conduct and quarrels during the event.<ref name=":2" />

=== 1756 (Hōreki 6) ===
The 7th domain lord, Tsugaru Nobunaga, viewed neighborhood "Nebuta" from the Three Enclosure Palace.<ref name=":2" />

=== 1776 (An'ei 5), July 2 (Lunisolar: August 15) ===
A restriction was placed limiting "Nebuta" processions to within their own neighborhood boundaries. Bringing neighborhood markers to other areas and instigating conflicts was prohibited.<ref name=":2" />

=== 1813 (Bunka 10), June 28 (Lunisolar: July 25) ===
Any "Nebuta" exceeding three shaku (about 1 meter) in height was strictly prohibited, even for children. The use of large drums was also restricted.<ref name=":2" />

=== 1828 (Bunsei 11), July 7 (Lunisolar: August 17) ===
The 10th domain lord, Tsugaru Nobuyuki, viewed the "Ito-tori Ningyō Nebuta," a Nebuta featuring a puppet of a woman spinning thread from a cocoon.<ref name=":3">"Fūnai Jiji-en"</ref>

=== 1830 (Tenpō 1), July 5-6 (Lunisolar: August 22-23) ===
A massive "Nebuta" from Watoku-machi was too large to pass through Konyamachi, attracting many spectators. Tsugaru Juntoku (later the 11th domain lord, Tsugaru Yukinari) from Kuroishi Clan also observed the event.<ref name=":3" />

=== 1842 (Tenpō 13), June 12 (Lunisolar: July 29) ===
A ban was imposed on elaborate "Nebuta" exceeding three shaku in height, large drums, extravagant festival costumes, and daytime performances.<ref name=":2" />

=== 1867 (Keiō 3), July 6 (Lunisolar: August 5) ===
A large "Nebuta" was built in various neighborhoods, but due to poor harvests in previous years, officials ordered the dismantling of large neighborhood-built "Nebuta."<ref name=":2" />

=== 1881 (Meiji 14), September ===
Emperor Meiji observed "Nebuta."<ref name=":11" />

This document continues detailing the historical regulations and modifications of the Nebuta festival through the Meiji and Showa periods, including its prohibition during wartime and subsequent revival.

== See Also ==
